findings.title: Dock app layout is restored through macOS defaults even when the Dock layout wizard choice is off
findings.suggested_fix: Treat the macOS Dock preference category as settings-only by removing app-layout keys such as persistent-apps, persistent-others, recent-apps, and recent-apps from the exported com.apple.dock plist, or split Dock app layout into the existing explicit Dock selection only. Add a generation regression where dock.backup is false and macos_preferences includes dock, then assert install.sh cannot restore Dock app order via defaults import.

findings.title: Default app export drops non-all LaunchServices roles
findings.suggested_fix: Export LSHandlerRoleAll, LSHandlerRoleViewer, LSHandlerRoleEditor, and LSHandlerRoleShell separately, mapping them to duti roles all/viewer/editor/shell. Keep the bundle id as the first column, the content type or URL scheme as the second column, and the mapped duti role as the third column; add a fixture-style regression with viewer/editor-only handlers to prove install.sh emits the expected duti invocations.

residual_risks: Review was read-only and scoped to the provided diff; no interactive wizard or generated install script was executed in a real PTY.
macOS defaults domains are OS-version sensitive, so some exported domains may be absent or structurally different across supported macOS releases.
testing_gaps: No regression asserts that selecting macOS Dock settings without selecting Dock layout preserves the user's explicit Dock-layout skip.
No fixture covers LaunchServices handlers that use LSHandlerRoleViewer, LSHandlerRoleEditor, or LSHandlerRoleShell without LSHandlerRoleAll.
No agent/automation-facing non-interactive fixture verifies the selections JSON for recommended versus custom macOS preference choices.
